completed_slices.summary: Centralized terminal reasons and live status interpretation in the daemon-owned feed projection, made CLI painters render RunDetails.feed, documented the projection authority, and kept run/slice status enums unchanged.
public_interfaces_changed: RunDetails status JSON now exposes primary_terminal_reason for terminal blocked/failed/cancelled/interrupted runs.
RunDetails.feed is the versioned daemon-side StatusFeed projection and includes feed.terminal_reason plus feed.operator_commands.
TerminalReason and StatusFeed fields are serialized as daemon status JSON consumed by CLI/Pi painters.

acceptance_status.criterion: Blocked and failed runs expose a structured primary terminal reason with kind, resolution owner, retryability or operator-action flags where applicable, evidence links, and remediation or disposition text.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/domain.rs defines TerminalReason; src/daemon.rs derives primary_terminal_reason from structured incident/run-error data or terminal fallback; cargo test terminal_reason --quiet and monitor_specific_run_returns_error_for_failed_terminal_status verified blocked auth and failed-run terminal reasons with kind, owner, retryability/operator-action flags, evidence, remediation/disposition surfaces, and commands.

acceptance_status.criterion: RunDetails/status JSON includes one versioned daemon-side feed projection that carries terminal reasons, progress, incidents, economics summary, and exact operator commands needed for questions or replan decisions.
acceptance_status.evidence: Server::run_details now sets details.feed = Some(project_run(&details)); src/workflow/projection.rs emits feed_version 1 with terminal_reason, operator_commands, progress, incidents, economics, attention, and commands blocks. cargo test projection --quiet and status_and_watch_expose_live_progress_for_long_verification --quiet passed.

acceptance_status.criterion: CLI status, watch, and monitor render feed blocks as painters and no longer match raw daemon event-type strings to decide human wording.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/cli.rs watch and monitor render RunDetails.feed blocks or report feed unavailability; raw event interpretation was removed from CLI painters. The required rg guard passed with no matches in src/cli.rs.

acceptance_status.criterion: The Pi feedback adapter consumes the same feed projection and does not independently infer workflow state from raw events or process/session lifecycle.
acceptance_status.evidence: The existing khazad-worker Pi adapter renders details.feed.summary_line, attention, and blocks from daemon status JSON and does not read raw events; npm test passed, including the khazad attach daemon-feed rendering coverage.

acceptance_status.criterion: Existing monitor behavior remains equivalent on the recorded-run corpus except for deliberate wording changes documented in the slice result.
acceptance_status.evidence: cargo test monitor --quiet and monitor_specific_run_returns_error_for_failed_terminal_status --quiet passed. Deliberate wording/schema changes are documented in README.md, docs/workflow-invariants.md, and skills/khazad-doom/SKILL.md as Terminal and Commands feed blocks plus feed-owned terminal reason/operator command wording.

acceptance_status.criterion: No new run or slice status values are introduced.
acceptance_status.evidence: RunStatus and SliceStatus variants/parsers remain unchanged in src/domain.rs; terminal classification is represented by TerminalReason/feed fields instead of new statuses. The slice validation and targeted cargo tests passed.

assumptions: The existing ./extensions/khazad-worker package is the Pi feedback adapter in this checkout; the listed extensions/khazad-monitor path is not present, so no extension path rename or out-of-area adapter edit was made.
Monitor wording changes are intentionally limited to daemon feed block wording, especially Terminal and Commands blocks.

completed_slices.summary: Implemented the Herdr cockpit seam with auto/default fallback behavior, durable and per-run cockpit mode overrides, non-fatal cockpit incident recording, docs/skill updates, real-Herdr smoke coverage, and repaired the clippy too_many_arguments failure in run_resume.
public_interfaces_changed: Added durable .workflow/khazad.json cockpit mode values: auto, herdr, direct
Added daemon events cockpit_ready and non-fatal cockpit_unavailable incident payloads

acceptance_status.criterion: A small Cockpit/CockpitAdapter seam exists for opening or focusing a run workspace and creating named read-only panes; callers do not learn Herdr command details, pane ids beyond opaque references, or terminal-layout mechanics.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/workflow/cockpit.rs adds Cockpit, CockpitAdapter, opaque workspace/pane refs, and named pane requests; workflow callers only call record_cockpit_launch/open_default_run_cockpit.

acceptance_status.criterion: The default cockpit mode is auto: when a usable herdr binary is found, a run attempts to create or focus a workspace named for the run and opens a Run Status / Event Feed pane plus a read-only Integration Gate / Repair phase pane; when Herdr is unavailable or fails, the run falls back to current direct execution.
acceptance_status.evidence: WorkflowConfig defaults cockpit to auto; CockpitRunRequest names workspaces Khazad-Doom <run-id>; open_run creates Run Status / Event Feed and Integration Gate / Repair panes; failures return cockpit_unavailable while execution continues.

acceptance_status.criterion: Operators can override cockpit behavior through durable config and per-run CLI flag values equivalent to auto, herdr, or direct.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/domain.rs adds CockpitMode and config field; src/cli.rs adds --cockpit auto|herdr|direct for run/resume; tests cover direct CLI overriding durable herdr config.

acceptance_status.criterion: Herdr cockpit failures are recorded as non-fatal cockpit-unavailable incidents or feed entries with remediation, and they never change slice selection, worker authorization, verification, merge, handoff, or terminal run status by themselves.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/workflow/manager.rs records run_incident kind cockpit_unavailable with remediation/fallback/source_of_truth and does not alter run status; cockpit_herdr_failure_is_nonfatal_incident passes.

acceptance_status.criterion: The Planner Pi pane is explicitly deferred until RPL planner behavior exists; HERDR-01 does not launch a planner agent.
acceptance_status.evidence: Cockpit setup only creates feed and integration/repair panes; cockpit tests assert no planner commands, and cockpit_ready payload marks planner as deferred.

acceptance_status.criterion: Real-Herdr smoke evidence proves workspace creation/focus and feed/phase pane creation on an installed Herdr, while the normal full test suite remains portable when Herdr is absent.
acceptance_status.evidence: herdr --version reported herdr 0.7.1; HERDR_E2E=1 cargo test herdr_cockpit_workspace_real --quiet passed and checks workspace plus both pane labels; regular cockpit tests also pass without requiring Herdr.

findings.description: Replaced run_resume's eight-argument signature with ResumeCliOptions; clippy now passes with -D warnings.

assumptions: Herdr remains an observability launcher/painter only; daemon state remains the source of truth.
The Planner Pi pane is intentionally deferred to later RPL planner-authority slices.

completed_slices.summary: Implemented Herdr-backed Pi worker panes while keeping Khazad-Doom authoritative: deterministic worker pane labels, KD-owned wrapper artifacts, Pi contract parsing from captured stdout, daemon-owned cancellation/fallback behavior, docs updates, real-Herdr smoke coverage, and clippy/env-isolation repairs.
completed_slices.commit_message: khazad: isolate herdr wrapper pi env
public_interfaces_changed: src/agent.rs: added PiCommandSpec and Runner::pi_command_spec() so the workflow manager can reuse the resolved Pi command for wrapped Herdr launches

acceptance_status.criterion: For Herdr-backed runs, each active slice worker is visible in a deterministically named Herdr pane tied to the run id and slice id, but Khazad-Doom still decides which slice is active and which worker command is authorized.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/workflow/cockpit.rs creates worker panes named `Worker {run_id}/{slice_id} attempt {attempt}`; src/workflow/manager.rs builds the authorized worker request from the daemon-selected run/slice/attempt. Covered by `cargo test cockpit --quiet` and `HERDR_E2E=1 cargo test herdr_worker_wrapper_real --quiet`.

acceptance_status.criterion: Herdr worker panes execute a KD-owned wrapper that launches Pi with the same resolved provider/model/profile/env/prompt/schema as the direct Pi runner and writes stdout, stderr, exit status, and result files under the run artifact directory.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/agent.rs exposes the direct Pi command spec, writes prompt/env/command/wrapper artifacts, launches Pi via the wrapper, captures stdout/stderr/exit/result files, and isolates the launched Pi environment with `env -i` plus KD-written env data. Real-Herdr smoke verified artifact creation.

acceptance_status.criterion: Khazad-Doom parses worker results from KD-owned artifact files using the same Pi contract parser and JSON-only result validation as direct Pi execution; Herdr terminal rendering, pane scrollback, and agent-status metadata are never correctness inputs.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/agent.rs `parse_pi_artifact_result` reads wrapper stdout/stderr artifacts, uses `PiParser`, and applies the same JSON extraction path as direct Pi. README, docs/workflow-invariants.md, and skill docs state pane text/scrollback/Herdr metadata are non-authoritative. `cargo test pi_contract --quiet` passed.

acceptance_status.criterion: Cancellation remains daemon-owned: cancelling a run requests termination of the wrapped Pi process and records the same terminal/cancellation evidence as the direct runner path.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/agent.rs `collect_pi_wrapper_result` observes the daemon cancellation token, terminates the wrapped Pi pid with the configured grace path, and returns a RunnerError transcript through the existing supervised worker pipeline. `cargo test cancelled_pi_worker_retains_terminal_and_attempt_artifacts_black_box --quiet` passed.

acceptance_status.criterion: If Herdr pane startup or wrapper handoff fails before a worker is launched, the run falls back to the existing direct Pi runner and records a non-fatal cockpit fallback incident; if the wrapped Pi worker itself fails, the failure is treated like a normal worker failure, not hidden as a cockpit fallback.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/workflow/manager.rs separates `CockpitWorkerJobError::Fallback` from `Worker`, records `cockpit_worker_fallback` only for pre-launch cockpit/wrapper handoff failures, and otherwise returns worker errors. `cargo test cockpit --quiet` covered fallback incidents.

acceptance_status.criterion: Real-Herdr evidence proves a worker pane can run the wrapper and return a valid worker JSON result through artifact capture; normal cargo test remains portable unless the explicit Herdr e2e test is requested.
acceptance_status.evidence: `HERDR_E2E=1 cargo test herdr_worker_wrapper_real --quiet` passed with Herdr on PATH, verifying pane label and wrapper artifacts. The test skips unless HERDR_E2E=1, keeping normal cargo tests portable.

findings.description: Removed the useless anyhow::Error conversion reported by clippy.
Adjusted the wrapper to launch Pi under a sanitized environment rebuilt from KD-owned env artifacts so pane-only env does not affect worker execution.

assumptions: No must_ask_if fence was hit because worker JSON remains read only from KD-owned artifacts, Herdr remains non-authoritative, direct Pi fallback remains intact, and the Pi JSON contract was not changed or relaxed.

completed_slices.summary: Implemented a thin Pi/Herdr bridge: added the khazad-monitor extension with feed/explain/open/handoff/answer actions, added `khazad-doom cockpit open` for run/latest Herdr open/focus with fallback JSON, updated docs/package metadata, and added integration/extension tests.
completed_slices.commit_message: khazad(slice:HERDR-03): open herdr cockpit from pi bridge
public_interfaces_changed: Pi package extension path now declares ./extensions/khazad-monitor

acceptance_status.criterion: The Pi adapter surface is documented and implemented as a thin bridge: start or shape a run, explain current run state from daemon feed/handoff data, answer operator blockers through daemon commands, summarize handoff, and open or focus the Herdr cockpit for a run.
acceptance_status.evidence: extensions/khazad-monitor registers ask_operator plus explicit /khazad-attach, /khazad-explain, /khazad-open, /khazad-handoff, and /khazad-answer commands; README/skill docs describe Pi starting/shaping via daemon CLI and bridge-only actions.

acceptance_status.criterion: The Pi adapter does not render or emulate a full live multi-agent cockpit, does not own process/session lifecycle, and does not infer workflow state from Pi session lifecycle.
acceptance_status.evidence: The adapter renders only a compact daemon feed widget/snapshot, delegates Herdr focus to the CLI, and session shutdown only clears Pi UI resources; docs/invariants explicitly reject a peer Pi cockpit.

acceptance_status.criterion: An explicit command or adapter action can open/focus the Herdr workspace for the latest or specified run and reports a clear fallback/remediation message when Herdr is unavailable.
acceptance_status.evidence: src/cli.rs adds `khazad-doom cockpit open --run` and `--latest --repo`; /khazad-open delegates to it. tests/daemon_integration.rs covers real open/focus and unavailable-Herdr fallback JSON with remediation/operator_commands.

acceptance_status.criterion: All live status text shown through Pi comes from the daemon-owned feed projection added by FEED-01; no raw event-type interpretation is duplicated in the Pi adapter.
acceptance_status.evidence: extensions/khazad-monitor renderRunFeed consumes RunDetails.feed and status text uses feed.summary_line; the Node test injects a raw event string and asserts it is not rendered.

acceptance_status.criterion: README, package metadata, and skill docs present the intended model: Pi starts/shapes/explains, Khazad-Doom owns run truth, Herdr shows and focuses live agents.
acceptance_status.evidence: README.md, package.json, and skills/khazad-doom/SKILL.md now describe khazad-monitor as a thin bridge, daemon-owned truth/status, and Herdr as the optional live cockpit.

acceptance_status.criterion: Existing operator escalation behavior remains intact: answering blockers goes through daemon state and token-scoped worker questions, not through manual typing in Herdr worker panes.
acceptance_status.evidence: ask_operator still calls daemon workerAsk with KHAZAD_WORKER_TOKEN; /khazad-answer calls daemon answerQuestion; docs state not to answer by typing in Herdr worker panes; npm tests cover workerAsk forwarding.

assumptions: Pi start/shape remains the khazad-doom skill invoking daemon CLI commands; the extension adds bridge helpers rather than a separate run lifecycle owner.
